title: Taskbar Alignment Tool Configuration
description: Configuration file for the Taskbar Alignment Tool. Controls resolution-based taskbar profile switching.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion.

refreshIntervalMs.description: Fallback polling interval in milliseconds. The app primarily uses Windows display-change events; this is a safety-net timer. Minimum 5000ms.

showNotifications.description: Show a balloon notification when the active profile changes.

notificationDurationMs.description: How long the profile-switch notification stays visible, in milliseconds.

resolutionMode.description: How to measure display width. 'Effective' divides physical pixels by the DPI scale factor (e.g., 3840px at 150% = 2560px). 'Physical' uses raw pixel count.

profiles.description: Resolution profiles ordered by minWidth. The profile with the highest minWidth <= current display width is activated.

name.description: Display name for this profile (shown in tray menu and notifications).

minWidth.description: Minimum effective display width (in pixels) to activate this profile. Use 0 for the fallback profile.

alignment.description: Taskbar alignment. Maps to registry key TaskbarAl (0=Left, 1=Center).

combineButtons.description: When to combine taskbar buttons. Maps to registry key TaskbarGlomLevel (0=Always, 1=WhenFull, 2=Never).

taskbarSize.description: Taskbar size. Maps to registry key TaskbarSi (0=Small, 1=Default, 2=Large). Hidden Windows 11 setting.
